---
title: "Edible Plants Dataset Analisis (TidyTuesday) - Data Cleaning and Preparation"
author: "Leonardo Cárdenas Peralta, Daniel Enrique Molina Sanglade and Enrique Fernandez Salmeron"
date: "April-May 2026"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
---

# Introduction

# Introduction

## Setup
```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Cargamos las librerías necesarias para el análisis.
# Nota: La gestión de versiones se realiza mediante renv.
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytuesdayR)
library(janitor)
library(skimr)
library(here)
library(tidymodels)
library(performance)
library(kableExtra)
```

## Downloading the dataset

Now we have to downlaod the dataset to start working with it

```{r Option 1, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2026-02-03')

edible_plants <- tuesdata$edible_plants
```

```{r Option 2, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
edible_plants <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-02-03/edible_plants.csv')
edible_plants
```

```{r Option 3, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Used to upload the file to Github

download.file(
  url = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-02-03/edible_plants.csv',
  destfile = "Data/edible_plants.csv"
)

# Used to have the dataset locally from Github

edible_plants <- read_csv(here("Data", "edible_plants.csv"))
```

# Data Cleaning and Preparation

In this section we clean the original dataset to correct inconsistencies and transform text variables into analyzable numerical data.

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 1. Name normalization and cleaning

# We are using janitor to parse the name of the categorical variables so they all have the same structure.
# Then we are using tidyverse to edit the content of the columns
edible_plants_clean <- edible_plants %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~str_trim(str_to_lower(.))))
```

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 2. Correcting inconsistencies and typo-errors
# This is going to help in the misspelling of "perrenial" and "perennial"
edible_plants_clean <- edible_plants_clean %>%
  mutate(
    cultivation = recode(cultivation, "brassicas" = "brassica"),
    season = case_when(
      str_detect(season, "perrenial|perannial") ~ "perennial",
      str_detect(season, "biennial") ~ "biennial",
      TRUE ~ season
    ),
    temperature_class = recode(temperature_class, "very hard" = "very hardy"),
    sunlight = str_replace_all(sunlight, "full sun/partial shade/ full shade", "full sun/partial shade/full shade")
  )
```

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 3. Transformation from ranges to numerico columns
# This feature extracts numbers from strings like "55-75" or "180+"
stats_extraction <- function(column) {
  nums <- str_extract_all(column, "\\d+")
  min_val <- sapply(nums, function(x) if(length(x) > 0) min(as.numeric(x)) else NA)
  max_val <- sapply(nums, function(x) if(length(x) > 0) max(as.numeric(x)) else NA)
  avg_val <- (min_val + max_val) / 2
  return(data.frame(min = min_val, max = max_val, avg = avg_val))
}

# We apply the function to the 4 main variables to create 12 new clean columns
stats_harvest <- stats_extraction(edible_plants$days_harvest)
stats_germ <- stats_extraction(edible_plants$days_germination)
stats_temp_germ <- stats_extraction(edible_plants$temperature_germination)
stats_temp_grow <- stats_extraction(edible_plants$temperature_growing)

edible_plants_clean <- edible_plants_clean %>%
  mutate(
    days_harvest_min = stats_harvest$min,
    days_harvest_max = stats_harvest$max,
    days_harvest_avg = stats_harvest$avg,
    days_germ_min = stats_germ$min,
    days_germ_max = stats_germ$max,
    days_germ_avg = stats_germ$avg,
    temp_germ_min = stats_temp_germ$min,
    temp_germ_max = stats_temp_germ$max,
    temp_germ_avg = stats_temp_germ$avg,
    temp_grow_min = stats_temp_grow$min,
    temp_grow_max = stats_temp_grow$max,
    temp_grow_avg = stats_temp_grow$avg
  )
```

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 4. Empyt values management
# We replace Nas with categorical variables so we do not loss information in the charts
edible_plants_clean <- edible_plants_clean %>%
  mutate(
    soil = replace_na(soil, "not specified"),
    nutrients = replace_na(nutrients, "not specified")
  )
```

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# 5. Changes verification
# We compare the number of columns of the original model vs the new one
cat("Original columns:", ncol(edible_plants), "\n")
cat("Columns after cleaning:", ncol(edible_plants_clean), "\n")

# We show the first rows of the new added columns
edible_plants_clean %>% 
  select(common_name, starts_with("days_harvest")) %>% 
  head() %>%
  kable() %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
```
